name: Halloween Makeup
recipeIngredient: 1/4 Teaspoon Liquid Food Coloring (Color of Choice) OR
1/4 Teaspoon Paste Food Coloring (Color of Choice)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small mixing bowl, combine the cornstarch and flour.
2. Gradually stir in the Karo corn syrup and water until smooth.
3. Divide the mixture into 4 portions for each child's face.
4. Color 3 portions with desired food coloring (use 1/2 teaspoon liquid or 1/4 teaspoon paste per batch).
5. Leave one portion untinted.
For additional colors:
6. Tear tissues into single layers and then lengthwise into 2-inch wide strips.
7. Paint a generous coat of the untinted mixture on the forehead.
8. Place a strip of tissue over the area and pat firmly with fingertips.
9. Brush on more of the same mixture to cover any dry spots on the tissue.
10. Continue painting the rest of the face, one area at a time, covering it with tissue.
Do not paint eyelids or around eyes. Allow to dry for about 15 minutes; this time may vary depending on temperature and humidity.
Before using colored batches, stir them well. Apply colors one at a time as desired.
Allow to slightly dry before using another color if blending is desired, or allow to completely dry if not.
For a deeper, smoother candy-colored effect, apply several coats of the same color, allowing it to slightly dry between coats.
To build up eyebrows, nose, or chin (or for a beard), apply cotton to damp, untinted mixture and paint over it.
For a ghoulish effect, dust the made-up face with flour.
For warts, stick Puffed Wheat cereal into the untinted mixture, then coat with layers of mixture-soaked tissue.
To remove makeup, wet the face and gently peel away the tissue. Wash the face with warm water.
